extraction_reasoning: The litigation table separates matters filed by and against each category: I counted only the “Against” rows, with criminal proceedings against the company, directors, promoters, subsidiaries, KMP and senior management all Nil (0); statutory/regulatory actions against the Company (3) and Directors (1) total 4; and tax proceedings against the Company (15) plus Subsidiaries (Nil) total 15. For the aggregate amount against the Company and Subsidiaries only, I counted the Company’s quantified amount of ₹44.71 crore and the Subsidiaries’ Nil amount, excluding the ₹0.13 crore promoter amount and all “By” rows. The contingent-liabilities table is dated September 30, 2025 and has no stated Total row, so I summed its disclosed line items: ₹0.01 + ₹1.34 + ₹9.30 + ₹0.37 + ₹0.03 + ₹16.25 + ₹0.98 + ₹12.29 = ₹40.57 crore; the table states that subsidiary contingent liabilities are included on a consolidated basis. The section provides only business-segment concentration percentages, such as Generator Set Business revenue and supplier concentration as a percentage of total expenses, and no company-wide top-five or top-ten customer percentage for a full fiscal year; therefore those fields are null. The litigation and contingent-liability source unit is ₹crore, so no conversion is required (₹1 crore = ₹10 million = ₹100 lakh).
